content:
📜 Rules for Time Progression
✔ If class starts in Day Mode, formal argument structures & order apply first before we enter literary disorder.
✔ If class starts in Night Mode, abstract thought reigns first, and by the end, everything must be made concrete.
✔ Some students (e.g., The Trickster) may have the ability to reverse the order for the day.

✅ Why This Works:
• Mirrors the literary structures we are studying.
• Forces students to engage in different cognitive modes.
• Ties setting and gameplay to structured vs. fragmented narratives.

📜 2. The Weekly Boss – A Legendary Figure from Literature & Mythology

📜 Each week ends with a literary “boss” that students must defeat, outwit, or answer.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 218 (chunk 218/555)
